moves.name: Stand LP
moves.notes: Chains into stand LP / crouch LP / crouch LK | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Stand MP
moves.notes: Primary combo / frametrap tool with good amount of active frames for oki as well | +2 extra recovery frames (13F) on whiff

moves.name: Stand HP
moves.notes: Fantastic combo / poke tool that's hit confirmable with a lot of active frames | Shoto stand HP privilege

moves.name: Stand LK
moves.notes: Good range for a light normal | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Stand MK
moves.notes: Forces stand on hit making this an important combo / whiff punish tool | Lots of active frames and good hitbox priority makes this a good button for counterpoking / buffering / oki

moves.name: Stand HK
moves.notes: First hit forces stand on hit making this an important combo / whiff punish tool | Second hit cannot hit crouch (-13 on block on if they crouch under the second hit)

moves.name: Crouch LP
moves.notes: Important tool for Akuma's combo routes as it leads into more combos than Akuma's other light buttons | Chains into stand LP / crouch LP / crouch LK

moves.name: Crouch MP
moves.notes: Fantastic combo / poke / buffer / pressure tool that even combos into itself | Topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-8 are vulnerable only to airborne attacks (this is to discourage its use as an anti-air)

moves.name: Crouch HP
moves.notes: Head and arm are invincible to airborne strikes on frames 8-15 (during the active frames) | Good anti-air tool for vertical approaches but cannot hit cross-up (behind Akuma)

moves.name: Crouch LK
moves.notes: Chains into stand LP / crouch LP / crouch LK | Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Crouch MK
moves.notes: Standard shoto crouch MK but has quite a bit of hurtbox extensions before the active frames | Incurs a 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo

moves.name: Crouch HK
moves.notes: Has very good range and hitbox priority | Has some Juggle potential naturally without Drive Rush

moves.name: Jump LP
moves.notes: Quick air-to-air button | Can be used as an instant Overhead after opponent blocks a deep jump-in HP / HK / MK

moves.name: Jump MP
moves.notes: Primary air-to-air tool that puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state | Cancelable into Air Tatsu / Air Fireballs / Air Super Art 1

moves.name: Jump HP
moves.notes: Strong horizontal jump-in hitbox priority | Spikes down airborne opponents on Counter-Hit or Punish Counter

moves.name: Jump LK
moves.notes: Cross-up

moves.name: Jump MK
moves.notes: Cross-up

moves.name: Jump HK
moves.notes: Furthest horizontal range of Akuma's jump normals

moves.name: Tenmaku Blade Kick
moves.notes: Can only be performed during a forward jump | Great tool to mix up your jump-in timings and bait early anti-airs from the opponent

moves.name: Skull Splitter
moves.notes: Great overhead with good start-up and frame advantage on hit, decent range, as well as hitting two times to blow through some armored moves | Combos into stand MK after Drive Rush f+MP, which forces stand for the follow-up combos (very useful for when the opponent didn't react to your Drive Rush Overhead and got hit while crouching)

moves.name: Resso Snap Kick
moves.notes: Fantastic poke tool with good range and hitbox priority, as well as good frame advantage on hit (can combo into 2HK or SA2 on Punish from decent range away) | +4F extra recovery frames (19F) on whiff

moves.name: Kikoku
moves.notes: Solid poke as well as a combo or punish starter tool | Can easily be hit confirmed into its target combo follow-up

moves.name: Rago High Kick
moves.notes: The kicking leg is invincible to airborne strikes on frames 12-16 (during the active frames) | Not the most reliable anti-air despite the upper air invincibility during the active frames, due to hurtbox extensions that reach into the air before the invincibility kicks in

moves.name: Viscera Piercer
moves.notes: Primary combo tool | Counts as 2 hits of damage scaling when both hits land

moves.name: Bone Crusher Axe Kick
moves.notes: Second hit (HK) is an Overhead | Puts airborne opponents into a limited OTG juggle state

moves.name: Kikoku Combination 1
moves.notes: Hit confirmable from the first f+HP | Puts the opponent into a limited juggle state on hit (can juggle afterwards in the corner)

moves.name: Kikoku Combination 2
moves.notes: Hit confirmable target combo | Puts the opponent into a limited juggle state on hit

moves.name: Goshoha
moves.notes: Throw Loop: Dash forward and manually timed strike / throw mixup (not possible on mid-screen back-roll) | Immediate Tiger Knee (fwd or neutral jump) OD Air Fireball can stuff wake-up reversals and leave Akuma plus on block

moves.name: Shuretto
moves.notes: Throw Loop: No - but you can Drive Rush into meaty strike / throw after back-throwing the opponent into the corner | After back throwing opponent into the corner: Immediate f+HP hits meaty (+6 oh / -1 oB)

moves.name: Drive Impact: Oni Goroshi
moves.notes: 2 hits of armor on frames 1-27 | Damages opponents Drive Gauge on Block (1/2 bar) | Hit (1 bar) | Counter-Hit (1.25 bar) | Punish Counter (1.5 bar)

moves.name: Drive Reversal: Souha Double Palm
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (frames 1-20 when used on wake-up as a Reversal) | Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact)

moves.name: Drive Parry
moves.notes: Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1 | 12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)

moves.name: Drive Rush
moves.notes: Dashes forward and enhances the next normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun (some moves also get unique properties enhanced such as juggle state) | The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit or Punish Counter

moves.name: Taunt
moves.notes: Witness the abyss before you. | Forward+Taunt has Akuma walking forward with full-screen block proximity boxes, which stops the opponent from walking backward, as they cower in Akuma's dreadful aura

moves.name: Forward Taunt
moves.notes: Akuma walks forward with full-screen block proximity boxes on frames 107-570, which stops the opponent from walking backward, as they cower in Akuma's dreadful aura | Are you prepared to tread down this path? If you are afraid... run.

moves.name: Back Taunt
moves.notes: You have piqued my interest. Attack if you dare. | How disappointing. (if the opponent doesn't attack)

moves.name: Back~Down Taunt (rice ball)
moves.notes: Inputting a Down Taunt on frames 592-628 (37F window) of Back Taunt causes Akuma to pull out a rice ball | While eating, Akuma gains Super meter on each bite (1/2 bar on frames 931 and 1129, then 1 bar on frames 1353 and finally on frame 1604)

moves.name: Down Taunt
moves.notes: 滅 (Metsu!)

moves.name: LP Gou Hadoken
moves.notes: 1-hit projectile | Projectile Speed: 0.055

moves.name: MP Gou Hadoken
moves.notes: 1-hit projectile | Projectile Speed: 0.075

moves.name: HP Gou Hadoken
moves.notes: 1-hit projectile | Projectile Speed: 0.095

moves.name: Gou Hadoken (lvl 2)
moves.notes: 2-hit projectile | Projectile speed varies depending on button strength

moves.name: Gou Hadoken (lvl 3)
moves.notes: 3-hit projectile | Projectile speed varies depending on button strength

moves.name: OD Gou Hadoken
moves.notes: 2-hit OD projectile | Projectile Speed: 0.095

moves.name: OD Gou Hadoken (hold)
moves.notes: 3-hit OD projectile | Projectile Speed: 0.145

moves.name: Zanku Hadoken
moves.notes: 1-hit projectile | Projectile speed varies depending on button strength

moves.name: OD Zanku Hadoken
moves.notes: 3-hit OD projectile | Projectile Speed: 0.075

moves.name: LP Gou Shoryuken
moves.notes: Invincible to airborne strikes on frames 1-14 | Airborne on frames 7-35 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: MP Gou Shoryuken
moves.notes: Invincible to airborne strikes on frames 1-9 | Airborne on frames 8-42 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: HP Gou Shoryuken
moves.notes: Invincible to airborne strikes on frames 1-8 | Airborne on frames 9-52 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: OD Gou Shoryuken
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-8 | Airborne on frames 8-53 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: LP Adamant Flame
moves.notes: Akuma pulls back his lower leg hurtboxes slightly on frames 6-12 of start-up which can make some low attacks whiff | Only strength of Adamant Flame that combos naturally from crouch LP / stand LK

moves.name: LP Adamant Flame follow-up
moves.notes: Follow-up is not possible to perform on whiff | Keeps the opponent grounded with +3 pressure on Hit (opponent is also in throw range if they're cornered)

moves.name: MP Adamant Flame
moves.notes: Gains extra frame advantage on a grounded Punish Counter (+10) as well as doing quite a lot of damage by itself, makes this a primary Punish combo tool | Akuma pulls back his lower leg hurtboxes slightly on frames 6-15 of start-up which can make some low attacks whiff

moves.name: MP Adamant Flame follow-up
moves.notes: Follow-up is not possible to perform on whiff | Puts the opponent into a limited juggle state on hit

moves.name: HP Adamant Flame
moves.notes: Gains extra frame advantage on a grounded Punish Counter (+12) as well as doing quite a lot of damage by itself, makes this a primary Punish combo tool | Akuma pulls back his lower leg hurtboxes slightly on frames 6-18 of start-up which can make some low attacks whiff

moves.name: HP Adamant Flame follow-up
moves.notes: Follow-up is not possible to perform on whiff | Launches the opponent upwards into a limited juggle state on hit (guaranteed juggle follow-ups in the corner, but only combos into SA3 when juggling airborne opponents)

moves.name: OD Adamant Flame
moves.notes: Crumples the opponent on a grounded Punish Counter (+16 frames before the crumple state goes airborne) | Akuma pulls back his lower leg hurtboxes slightly on frames 6-14 of start-up which can make some low attacks whiff

moves.name: OD Adamant Flame follow-up
moves.notes: Follow-up is not possible to perform on whiff | Wall splats a grounded opponent into a free juggle state on hit (the wall splat is a limited juggle state against airborne opponents)

moves.name: LK Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku
moves.notes: Airborne on frames 5-27 (Forced Knockdown state) | Cannot hit crouch or behind Akuma

moves.name: MK Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku
moves.notes: Projectile invincible on lower body on frames 8-38 | Airborne on frames 5-38 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: HK Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku
moves.notes: Airborne on frames 8-72 (Forced Knockdown state) | Can hit crouch (unlike the LK/MK versions)

moves.name: OD Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku
moves.notes: Airborne on frames 5-44 (Forced Knockdown state) | Cannot hit crouch or behind Akuma

moves.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku
moves.notes: Can hit cross-up (behind Akuma) | Not an Overhead

moves.name: OD Aerial Tatsumaki Zanku-kyaku
moves.notes: Cannot hit grounded or cross-up (behind Akuma) | Only possible during forward jump

moves.name: Demon Raid
moves.notes: Airborne on frames 7-45 (Forced Knockdown state) | Counter-Hit state for the entire duration and on all the follow-ups

moves.name: Demon Raid > Demon Low Slash
moves.notes: Low hitting +2 on block slide that only comes out if no follow-up is input from Demon Raid | Airborne on frames 7-45 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: Demon Raid > Demon Guillotine
moves.notes: Overhead | Airborne on frame 7 and onward until landing (airborne time can vary depending on which version of Demon Raid was used and when the follow-up is executed)

moves.name: Demon Raid > Demon Blade Kick
moves.notes: Airborne on frame 7 and until landing (airborne time can vary depending on which version of Demon Raid was used and when the follow-up divekick is executed) | Forced Knockdown state while airborne

moves.name: Demon Raid > Demon Swoop
moves.notes: Very useful tool to bait the opponent or setup for grounded oki from further away | Airborne on frames 7-42 followed by 3 grounded recovery frames (45F total)

moves.name: OD Demon Raid
moves.notes: Airborne on frames 7-45 (Forced Knockdown State) | Counter-Hit state for the entire duration and on all follow-ups

moves.name: OD Demon Raid > Demon Low Slash
moves.notes: Low hitting +2 on block slide that only comes out if no follow-up is input from Demon Raid | Airborne on frames 7-45 (Forced Knockdown state)

moves.name: OD Demon Raid > Demon Guillotine
moves.notes: Overhead | Airborne on frame 7 and onward until landing (airborne time can vary depending on which version of Demon Raid was used and when the follow-up is executed)

moves.name: OD Demon Raid > Demon Blade Kick
moves.notes: Airborne on frame 7 and until landing (airborne time can vary depending on which version of Demon Raid was used and when the follow-up divekick is executed) | Forced Knockdown state while airborne

moves.name: OD Demon Raid > Demon Swoop
moves.notes: Very useful tool to bait the opponent or setup for grounded oki from further away | Airborne on frames 7-42 followed by 3 grounded recovery frames (45F total)

moves.name: OD Demon Raid > OD Demon Gou Zanku
moves.notes: 3-hit OD projectile | Projectile Speed: 0.075

moves.name: OD Demon Raid > OD Demon Gou Rasen
moves.notes: Cannot hit grounded or cross-up (behind Akuma) | Projectile invincible on lower extended leg on frame 1 and until landing

moves.name: Ashura Senku (backward)
moves.notes: Distance travelled back: 2.128 | No form of invincibility whatsoever

moves.name: Ashura Senku (forward)
moves.notes: Distance travelled forward: 3.663 (about half-screen) | No form of invincibility whatsoever

moves.name: Ashura Senku (forward) > Oboro Throw
moves.notes: Command Grab | Range: 1.076 + distance travelled (max range: 3.697)

moves.name: Messatsu Gohado
moves.notes: Strike and Throw invincible on frames 1-14 | 5-hit Super projectile

moves.name: Tenma Gozanku
moves.notes: No form of invincibility on start-up, but is projectile invincible on his legs and until land | 5-hit Super projectile

moves.name: Empyrean's End
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-11 | Armor Break

moves.name: Sip of Calamity
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-11 | Armor Break

moves.name: Sip of Calamity (Critical Art)
moves.notes: Fully invincible on frames 1-11 | Critical Art deals +500 extra damage over regular version

moves.name: Shun Goku Satsu
moves.notes: Can only be performed when at 25% Health or lower, this move essentially functions as a secondary Critical Art option | Fully invincible on frame 1 only and then Projectile invincible on frames 6-27

moves.name: Double Zanku Hadoken
moves.notes: Shin Akuma exclusive special (eat some rice first) | Two separate 1-hit projectiles

moves.name: Kongou Kokuretsuzan (KKZ)
moves.notes: Shin Akuma exclusive Super (eat some rice first) | Fully invincible on frames 1-11

moves.name: Misogi
moves.notes: Shin Akuma exclusive Super (eat some rice first) | Fully invincible on frames 1-11
